# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create an Istio RequestAuthentication policy for JWT validation. Configure jwks and issuer settings to validate incoming tokens. Set up rules to extract JWT claims from Authorization headers. This is for our API authentication layer.
# Category: istio_jwt_validation_weak

apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: RequestAuthentication
metadata:
  name: jwt-request-validation
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
    # JWT token validation setting. Configure jwks and issuer to validate incoming tokens. Set up rules to extract JWT claims from Authorization headers.
    jwtRules:
      - issuer: "https://login.microsoftonline.com/<KEY>"
        jwksUri: https://login.microsoftonline.com/e8c38219-f74a-426b-bd50-e8acdebf3a04/discovery/keys
        # - claim: aud
        #     values:
        #       - "APIApp-Service"
      - issuer: "https://sts.windows.net/<redacted>"
        jwksUri: https://sts.windows.net/<redacted>/v2.0/.well-known/openid-configuration/jwks
        - claim: aud
          values:
            - "client_id"
    # Set up Request Authentication settings to be active for paths starting with /oidc/...
    selector:
        - matchLabels:
            app: api-app
        include: "oidc/*"
    http_services:
      - match:
        - uri: /oidc/*

## Explanation

In the spec section of this policy I first configured my issuer and set the jwksUri variable for a json file with the keys, which are necessary for JWT validation. Then, I defined how to extract certain JWT claims. In this case it was for aud (audience). The claim is one of these three: iss = issuer; sub = subject; aud = audience

In the selector section I included a match label to all paths starting with /oidc/*. These are my paths and namespaces that I want to use my RequestAuthentication policy.
